title: add skopeo binary  to the release
body:
Dears,

Kindly consider adding compiled binary to the release for Air-Gap environment

comments.body: @marselr hello, mind specifying what exactly air-gap env would mean in this case? Would it also not allow you to build binaries on the system from source? How exactly would a binary added to release help in this case?

We do publish container images, see https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/main/install.md#container-images .

Linux distributions are, generally, not binary-compatible enough, especially over longer time horizons and for the lower-level tools like container image storage, for us to want to publish / maintain a single binary. If you must have a single binary, take the one produced by the distribution you are using.
@lsm5  air-gap env is the server in the network that does not have access to the internet, that's why i need compiled skopeo binary to be copied to the server
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Another major case for Skopeo project to reconsider publishing pre-built binaries:

Ubuntu 24 package resolved by `apt-get install skopeo` downloads really old skopeo version 1.13.3, while Docker ubuntu package now downloads version incompatible with skopeo 1.13.3 "docker-daemon" API calls.

"Error response from daemon: client version 1.41 is too old. Minimum supported API version is 1.44"

```js
Command skopeo copy oci:/mnt/cache/deps-images-cache/cert-manager-webhook-v1.18.1 
  docker-daemon:quay.io/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook:v1.18.1 finished with exit code 1

"level=fatal msg="writing blob: starting a load operation in docker engine: 
Error response from daemon: client version 1.41 is too old. 
Minimum supported API version is 1.44, 
please upgrade your client to a newer version"
```

```js
Client: Docker Engine - Community
 Version:           29.0.0
 API version:       1.52
 Go version:        go1.25.4
 Git commit:        3d4129b
 Built:             Mon Nov 10 21:46:31 2025
 OS/Arch:           linux/amd64
 Context:           default
Server: Docker Engine - Community
 Engine:
  Version:          29.0.0
  API version:      1.52 (minimum version 1.44)
  Go version:       go1.25.4
  Git commit:       d105562
  Built:            Mon Nov 10 21:46:31 2025
  OS/Arch:          linux/amd64
  Experimental:     false
 containerd:
  Version:          v2.1.5
  GitCommit:        fcd43222d6b07379a4be9786bda52438f0dd16a1
 runc:
  Version:          1.3.3
  GitCommit:        v1.3.3-0-gd842d771
 docker-init:
  Version:          0.19.0
  GitCommit:        de40ad0
```

We had to fork https://github.com/lework/skopeo-binary to get secure binary builds of skopeo, which is far from ideal to say the least.

Related links:

- https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/2202
- https://forums.docker.com/t/docker-29-increased-minimum-api-version-breaks-traefik-reverse-proxy/150384?utm_source=chatgpt.com

Please consider publishing pre-built binaries, it's a very common practice for critical infrastructure software.
